title: Strata: Canvas Rendering at Scale (hybrid renderer benchmark)

files.name: chart.js
index.html
files.code:
Every SVG mark is a live DOM node, so a 50k-point scatter is roughly 100k nodes: slow to build and slow to restyle on hover. Strata keeps that DOM for the chart chrome but paints the dense series into a single <canvas>. Crank the points and flip the renderer to feel the difference.

Head to head · same data, both renderers

Press Benchmark both to render the current dataset once as SVG and once as canvas, and compare.

01Opt in, one line

Set chart.renderer: 'auto' and Strata switches to canvas once the mark count crosses rendererThreshold. Below it, output is identical SVG.

02Hybrid, not a rewrite

Only the series paths and markers move to canvas. Axes, gridlines, crosshairs, tooltips and annotations stay real SVG: crisp text, native events, SVG export.

03Tree shakeable

The interface and SVG adapter are core. The canvas backend is a separate feature, import 'apexcharts/features/renderer-canvas', that shakes out when unused.
